question.content: For the two-mass system rotating about point A, find the angular-momentum vector at the shown instant in the i1-j1-k1 basis. Answer format: a single vector expression. The variable names may only be Omega, phi, i1, j1, k1, m, and L; use ASCII characters only; do not include units, explanatory text, or an equals sign.

step_1.content: Use the inertia-tensor method in the i1-j1-k1 basis instead of direct velocity cross-product expansion.
step_1.key_point: Construct I_A first, then compute H by tensor projection.

step_3.content: Form inertia contributions of m1 and m2 about A, then assemble the combined inertia tensor with φ-dependent terms.
step_3.key_point: Diagonal and coupling tensor terms are identified before multiplying by Ω.

step_5.content: Project H = I_A*(Ω*k1) onto i1 and k1 to get component form directly.
step_5.key_point: Tensor projection gives the same final vector without repeating r x (omega x r).

step_7.content: Check symmetry requirement by adding the mirrored mass about x1-z1 plane so the coupling cancellation condition is explicit.
step_7.key_point: Symmetry check validates why only i1 and k1 components remain in the target expression.
